strategy.description: How to initialize Claude Code config: copy syncs host settings/plugins, fresh starts clean
strategy.title: Strategy

mount_projects.description: Bind mount host ~/.claude/projects/ into the container so auto-memory and sessions are shared across container runs and instances
mount_projects.title: Mount Host Projects

use_host_auth.description: Let the container use your host Claude Code credentials so you don't have to re-authenticate. The credential will be copied in at creation and persisted in the container's volume, but the container will refresh its tokens independently. If new containers keep starting unauthenticated, log in on the host to rotate the refresh token seed.
use_host_auth.title: Use Host Auth

editor.description: Editor for git commits and interactive editing inside the container
editor.title: Editor

enable_shared_dir.description: Share files between host and container via ~/.clawker-share (read-only in container)
enable_shared_dir.title: Enable Shared Dir

env.description: Set container env vars directly; use from_env to forward host values instead
env.title: Env

from_env.description: Pass specific host env vars into the container (e.g. AWS_PROFILE, GITHUB_TOKEN)
from_env.title: Forward Env Vars

post_init.description: Shell commands to run after container starts but before Claude Code launches (e.g. install MCP servers). Useful for seeding claude code config or running setup steps that require the container environment to be up. Runs only one time after container creation in the workdir with env vars loaded.
post_init.title: Post-Init Script

pre_run.description: Shell commands run on every container start, in the workdir, right before the CMD (default: claude) runs (e.g. npm install)
pre_run.title: Pre-Run Script

aliases.description: Command aliases expanded before execution; the value is appended to 'clawker' and supports $1..$N placeholders; merged across all config layers
aliases.title: Aliases

context.description: Directory to use as Docker build context when using a custom Dockerfile (relative to project root)
context.title: Build Context

dockerfile.description: Use your own Dockerfile instead of clawker's generated one; ignores image, packages, and instructions
dockerfile.title: Dockerfile

image.description: Starting Docker image (e.g. node:20-slim, python:3.12); clawker layers tools on top
image.title: Base Image

after_claude_install.description: Add Dockerfile instructions as the claude user with Claude Code available — e.g. add MCP servers, install plugins, or extensions
after_claude_install.title: After Claude Install

after_from.description: Add Dockerfile instructions while root with only the base image — e.g. apt sources, proxy config, or CA certs that package installation depends on
after_from.title: After FROM

after_packages.description: Add Dockerfile instructions while root with system packages available — e.g. compile native libraries or install tools that need those packages
after_packages.title: After Packages

after_user_setup.description: Add Dockerfile instructions while root with the claude user created — e.g. set up directories, fix permissions, or configure services
after_user_setup.title: After User Setup

after_user_switch.description: Add Dockerfile instructions as the claude user — e.g. install dotfiles, configure your shell, or set up user-level tools
after_user_switch.title: After User Switch

before_entrypoint.description: Add Dockerfile instructions at the very end — e.g. final environment tweaks or cleanup that must happen after everything else
before_entrypoint.title: Before Entrypoint

args.description: Build-time variables resolved during docker build (ARG); not available at runtime

default.description: Value used when not overridden by --build-arg at build time
default.title: Default

name.description: Build argument name (referenced as $NAME in Dockerfile instructions)
name.title: Name

args.title: Args

copy.description: Bake config files or credentials into the image (e.g. .npmrc, SSH config)

dest.description: Where to place it inside the container
dest.title: Destination

src.description: File or directory to copy from your project
src.title: Source

copy.title: Copy

env.description: Environment variables baked into the image; use agent.env for runtime-only vars
env.title: Env

labels.description: Custom Docker labels for image metadata or tooling integration
labels.title: Labels

root_run.description: Setup commands that need root privileges (e.g. system config, additional repos)
root_run.title: Root Run

user_run.description: Setup commands that run as the container user (e.g. npm install -g, pip install)
user_run.title: User Run

packages.description: System packages (apt/apk) needed by your project that aren't in the base image
packages.title: Packages

name.description: Override the project slug derived from the directory name (set this when the directory name isn't a good clawker identifier — e.g. dots, spaces, unicode)
name.title: Project Name

cap_add.description: Extra Linux capabilities for the agent container. Empty by default — the eBPF firewall is attached from outside, so no in-container caps are needed. Add e.g. SYS_PTRACE only if your workflow requires it.
cap_add.title: Cap Add

docker_socket.description: Mount the host Docker socket (DooD, not DinD) — lets the container manage sibling containers but is a security risk
docker_socket.title: Docker Socket

enable_host_proxy.description: Run a proxy for browser-based auth flows and credential forwarding from the host
enable_host_proxy.title: Host Proxy

add_domains.description: Shorthand: domains the container can reach over HTTPS (converted to https+port-443 rules)
add_domains.title: Firewall Domains

rules.description: Full egress rules with protocol, port, and path control

action.description: Allow or deny traffic to this destination (default: allow)
action.title: Action

dst.description: Domain or IP the container needs to reach (e.g. api.github.com, registry.npmjs.org)
dst.title: Destination

insecure_skip_tls_verify.description: Accept a self-signed/untrusted upstream TLS cert for this destination (default: false). Use only for trusted local-dev endpoints.
insecure_skip_tls_verify.title: Insecure Skip TLS Verify

path_default.description: What to do with HTTP paths that don't match any path rule (allow or deny)
path_default.title: Path Default

path_rules.description: Fine-grained path filtering (only applies to http/https/ws/wss)

action.description: Whether to allow or deny requests matching this path
action.title: Action

methods.description: HTTP methods this path rule applies to (e.g. GET, HEAD); empty = all methods. Only meaningful for http/https/ws/wss.
methods.title: Methods

path.description: URL path to match: a literal prefix starting with / (e.g. /v1/api), or — when prefixed with ~ — an RE2 regex matched full-string for exact/anchored matching (e.g. ~/repos/(a|b)/?)
path.title: Path

path_rules.title: Path Rules

port.description: Destination port: a single port (443) or an inclusive range (9000-9100); empty = protocol default
port.title: Port

proto.description: L7 protocol: https (TLS-MITM, default), http (plaintext HCM), ws/wss (websocket over http/https), ssh, tcp, udp, or any opaque L7 name for TCP pass-through
proto.title: Protocol

rules.title: Rules

copy_git_config.description: Sync your host .gitconfig (aliases, user.name, user.email) into the container
copy_git_config.title: Copy Git Config

forward_gpg.description: Let git sign commits using your host GPG keys
forward_gpg.title: Forward GPG

forward_https.description: Let git clone/push use your host HTTPS credentials (via host proxy)
forward_https.title: Forward HTTPS

forward_ssh.description: Let git use your host SSH keys for cloning and pushing
forward_ssh.title: Forward SSH

default_mode.description: bind mounts your project live (edits sync); snapshot copies it (isolated, disposable)
default_mode.title: Default Mode
